title: Agent Infrastructure Consolidation
scope: All 9 active Receptor ecosystem repositories (.agents/.agent directories)

findings.description: Each of the 9 repos maintains its own .agents/ directory with no symlink architecture. Skill updates in dev-environment never propagate automatically; local copies drift silently.

tasks.description: Create the 4 category subdirectories in dev-environment/.agents/: frontend-nextjs/, supabase-infrastructure/, backend-python/, docusaurus/ — each with workflows/ and rules/ subdirs.
Write canonical workflow files (audit-workflow.md, implement-audit-workflow.md, finalise-local-audit.md) into each of the 4 category directories, generalising repo-specific rows.
Delete local .agents/ workflow and rules contents from all 9 repos (keep directory for symlink step).

Create symlinks: planner-frontend/.agents -> dev-environment/.agents/frontend-nextjs, preference-frontend/.agents -> frontend-nextjs, workforce-frontend/.agents -> frontend-nextjs, website-frontend/.agents -> frontend-nextjs, supabase-receptor/.agents -> supabase-infrastructure, match-backend/.agents -> backend-python, receptor-planner/.agents -> backend-python, documentation/common-bond/.agents -> docusaurus.
Add .agents to .gitignore in each of the 9 child repos.

findings.description: dev-environment/.agents/ has no category-level organisation. Workflow files with category-specific context live in individual repos rather than in a shared canonical location. There is no frontend-nextjs, supabase-infrastructure, backend-python, or docusaurus category directory.

tasks.description: Addressed by ARCH-01-T1 and ARCH-01-T2 — no separate tasks required.

findings.description: planner-frontend has local copies of 10 skills (adversarial-code-review, design-md, enhance-prompt, find-skills, nextjs-app-router-patterns, playwright-best-practices, playwright-skill, react-components, stitch-loop, vitest, vitest-failure-investigation) all of which exist in dev-environment. Approximately 100+ files of pure duplication.

tasks.description: Delete frontend/planner-frontend/.agents/skills/ entirely (all 10 skill directories). These are addressed by the ARCH-01 symlink — skills are accessed via dev-environment.

findings.description: planner-frontend has rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md (mirrors dev-environment skill) and rules/pre-commit.md (repo-specific, may be legitimate). The vitest rule should be a required_skills reference, not a rule file.

tasks.description: Delete frontend/planner-frontend/.agents/rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md. Verify pre-commit.md content — if it references ecosystem-wide standards, move to category rules/; if repo-specific, it will be absorbed into the frontend-nextjs category.

findings.description: preference-frontend had a local copy of adversarial-code-review (confirmed identical to dev-environment). Deleted during this audit session.
preference-frontend has rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md (mirrors dev-environment skill) and rules/git.md. Both should be absorbed into the frontend-nextjs category rules.

tasks.description: rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md and rules/git.md will be absorbed into the frontend-nextjs/rules/ category in dev-environment (addressed by ARCH-01). Delete local copies after symlink is in place.

findings.description: workforce-frontend had a local copy of adversarial-code-review (confirmed identical). Deleted during this audit session.
workforce-frontend has rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md and rules/git.md. The vitest rule mirrors the canonical dev-environment skill.

tasks.description: rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md and rules/git.md will be absorbed into frontend-nextjs/rules/ (addressed by ARCH-01). Delete local copies after symlink.

findings.description: supabase-receptor had a local copy of adversarial-code-review (confirmed identical). Deleted during this audit session.

supabase-receptor has a rules file whose content mirrors the canonical dev-environment supabase-standards skill. Rules files are always-active unlike skills; this likely causes no functional harm but represents the rule-as-skill anti-pattern.

tasks.description: Delete supabase-receptor/.agents/rules/supabase-standards.md. The canonical skill at dev-environment/.agents/skills/supabase-standards/SKILL.md is referenced by absolute path in supabase-receptor's audit-workflow.md.

findings.description: supabase-postgres-best-practices (20+ reference files) exists only in common-bond's local .agents/skills/ and is not in dev-environment. Under the target architecture it should live in docusaurus/skills/ in dev-environment to be accessible to all docusaurus-category repos.

tasks.description: Copy documentation/common-bond/.agents/skills/supabase-postgres-best-practices/ into dev-environment/.agents/docusaurus/skills/supabase-postgres-best-practices/. Update the reference in docusaurus/workflows/audit-workflow.md to use the new canonical path.

findings.description: common-bond's audit-workflow.md (197 lines, dual-mode) contains Docusaurus-specific context not shared with any other documentation repo. Under the target architecture this file becomes the canonical docusaurus/workflows/audit-workflow.md in dev-environment.

tasks.description: Use common-bond's audit-workflow.md as the basis for dev-environment/.agents/docusaurus/workflows/audit-workflow.md. Generalise any common-bond-specific references. This is addressed as part of ARCH-01-T2.

findings.description: frontend/.agent/ exists at the monorepo parent level which is not a git repository. It contains 4 workflow files and 1 rules file. No agent auto-discovers this location. Its content (cross-frontend audit categories, A11y, performance guidance) is valuable but unreachable.

tasks.description: Absorb the unique cross-frontend content from frontend/.agent/workflows/audit-workflow.md into dev-environment/.agents/frontend-nextjs/workflows/audit-workflow.md. Then delete frontend/.agent/ entirely.

findings.description: documentation/receptor-ecosystem does not exist as a local repository. When created, it will need to be mapped to the docusaurus category symlink.

tasks.description: When receptor-ecosystem repo is created, create the symlink: documentation/receptor-ecosystem/.agents -> dev-environment/.agents/docusaurus. Add .agents to its .gitignore.

clarifications.decision: documentation/common-bond maps to the docusaurus category (not frontend-nextjs). A 4th category directory docusaurus/ is created in dev-environment to serve documentation repos.

frontend/ is not a git repository. The .agent/ directory is orphaned. Its useful content (cross-frontend audit categories) will be absorbed into frontend-nextjs/audit-workflow.md before the directory is deleted.

clarifications.item: Option A vs Option B symlink approach
clarifications.decision: Option A approved: full symlink of .agents/ to the appropriate category. Repo-specific context rows (stack, verify command) are generalised at category level; agents read actual repo files for specifics.

rules/vitest-failure-investigation.md files in planner, preference, and workforce frontends are the rule-as-skill anti-pattern. They will be deleted; the skill is referenced via absolute path in workflow required_skills.

implementationPhases.label: Create category structure and canonical workflows
Promote supabase-postgres-best-practices skill to docusaurus category
Delete local duplicates, create symlinks, update .gitignore

deferred.reason: documentation/receptor-ecosystem repository does not yet exist. This task applies when the repo is created.
